strat: Valencia, under Carlos Corberán (as of early 2026 analysis), often employs a 4-2-3-1 formation, focusing on defensive solidity and quick transitions. They tend to build up from the back, looking for quick central passes to bypass the opposition's press and exploit gaps. Their attacking play often involves pushing wingers into half-spaces while full-backs provide width, aiming for crosses into the box. They have shown struggles in goal-scoring in early 2026, often relying on individual quality in attacking transitions. Rayo Vallecano, historically under coaches like Andoni Iraola, is known for an intense, high-pressing, and high-octane style, often utilizing a 4-2-3-1 or 4-3-3 shape. They emphasize attacking width with overlapping full-backs and wingers who drift inside, creating overloads and aiming for crosses. Their midfield is crucial for ball control and counter-pressing.
